$comment: Degradation registry (#511 Part A). Machine-readable INDEX of every graceful-degradation mechanism in ARS: what fails, what degraded state is emitted, where the degradation is visible, who consumes it, and how the terminal-policy layer treats it. The registry INDEXES — it never re-authors: each row's `authority` points at the per-mechanism source of truth (a content anchor that must appear verbatim in the cited file), so semantics live in exactly one place and this file cannot drift into a second authority. Rows use each mechanism's NATIVE semantics (unresolvable / not_checked / PASS WITH NOTES / omit-field / warn-and-continue); there is deliberately NO universal score-cap column — ARS's gate model is tri-state outcomes + advisory suffixes + opt-in terminal policies, not a shared scalar score (#511 'Explicitly rejected: score capping'). Numeric/severity caps appear only where a scale already exists (see compliance_non_sr_warn_cap). Adding a row: fill every required field, cite the authority file with a short verbatim anchor (never a line number — anchors survive drift, line numbers do not), list the tests/lints/schemas that pin the behavior under `pinned_by` (path, or path::test_function), then run scripts/check_degradation_registry.py.

mechanisms.failure_class: Bibliographic lookup API unreachable (network error, 5xx, rate-limit exhaustion) during citation-existence verification
mechanisms.degraded_state: Per-resolver outcome status 'unreachable' (queried_by null); a TOTAL outage of all applicable resolvers reduces lookup_verified to 'unresolvable' — never to 'true' (an outage is not evidence of existence) and never alone to 'false' (an outage is not fabrication evidence)
mechanisms.diagnostic_marker: resolver_outcomes.<resolver>.status == 'unreachable' in the citation verification summary
mechanisms.downstream_consumer: pipeline_orchestrator_agent finalizer (5-cell gate) and terminal_policies.citation_existence evaluation
mechanisms.terminal_policy_effect: advisory by default; under citation_existence='strict' only lookup_verified=='false' (ID-keyed unmatched, C-V6(a)) refuses — 'unresolvable' from outage is NEVER blocked

mechanisms.failure_class: Lookup API (Semantic Scholar / OpenAlex / Crossref / arXiv) degraded while computing a contamination signal at ingest/backfill time
mechanisms.degraded_state: The per-resolver *_unmatched field is OMITTED from contamination_signals — never set to false (which would falsely read 'checked and clean'); the omission is excluded from the k/k_max triangulation count per R-L3-2-C
mechanisms.diagnostic_marker: contamination_signal_omissions.<signal> == 'api_degraded' on the corpus entry (#511 Part A reason-provenance; entries written before this field exists carry no marker — absence of BOTH the signal and the omission reason is indistinguishable from 'not yet computed', which is the pre-#511 live weakness this field closes)
mechanisms.downstream_consumer: pipeline_orchestrator_agent finalizer advisory-suffix matrix (k, k_max) per shared/references/firm_rules.md R-L3-2-C
mechanisms.terminal_policy_effect: advisory by default per R-L3-2-A; a degraded lookup is excluded from the k/k_max count (R-L3-2-C) and which annotation tier fires is determined by the resulting (k, k_max) pair — degradation can therefore change the tier — strengthen it, weaken it, or leave it unchanged — never monotonically weaken it; whether any tier is promoted to a terminal block is decided at the emission boundary by terminal_policies (opt-in strict, contamination_triangulation only), never by the omission itself

authority.anchor: None means OMIT the field from the
Absent fields are excluded from the count and do not default to either `true` or `false`.
By default, contamination and temporal-integrity signals never block emission on their own

mechanisms.failure_class: No multimodal/vision capability available for VLM figure verification (or figure too simple to warrant it)
mechanisms.degraded_state: VLM verification step is SKIPPED; the Figure Package records 'VLM verification: skipped' instead of a verification section
mechanisms.diagnostic_marker: 'VLM verification: skipped' note in the Figure Package output
mechanisms.downstream_consumer: integrity_verification_agent Stage 4.5 Phase C3 figure/table fidelity review
mechanisms.terminal_policy_effect: no terminal policy exists for VLM; a skipped verification can reach PASS WITH NOTES but never converts to FAIL on absence alone (FAIL requires a positive fidelity finding)

authority.anchor: If not available or figure is simple: skip (note "VLM verification: skipped" in Figure Package)
an entry reaches PASS WITH NOTES only when NO FAIL condition is met

mechanisms.failure_class: A submission-package check cannot run (missing artifact, unreadable input, extraction failure) during package verification
mechanisms.degraded_state: Check status 'not_checked' (distinct from pass AND from fail); process exit code 3 = 'passed what was checkable' — distinct from a full pass (0) and from a failure (1)
mechanisms.diagnostic_marker: [NOT-CHECKED] lines + not_checked_count in the verification report header; exit code 3
mechanisms.downstream_consumer: submission-package policy evaluation (advisory vs strict) and the orchestrator invoking scripts/verify_submission_package.py
mechanisms.terminal_policy_effect: advisory: not_checked surfaces but does not refuse (exit 3); under strict, ONLY strict_eligible not_checked checks fail closed (VERIFICATION-INCOMPLETE token, exit 4) — heuristic-classed not_checked stays nonterminal at exit 3 even under strict

authority.anchor: return 3  # "passed what was checkable" (§8) — distinct from a full pass
Fail-closed (§5.2): a missing parser/profile must not silently

mechanisms.failure_class: Transport-level failure of a CONFIGURED cross-model verifier mid-run (API error, rate limit, expired key). An unset ARS_CROSS_MODEL is NOT a degradation — it is the standard single-model configuration and produces no error marker or disclosure
mechanisms.degraded_state: Warn-and-continue: pipeline proceeds single-model; NEVER blocks on cross-model transport failure; a NOT_SEARCHED grounded-lookup result is NOT a transport failure and does not fall back (it is counted separately and surfaced for re-run/human review)
mechanisms.diagnostic_marker: [CROSS-MODEL-ERROR: reason] log line + required report disclosure 'Cross-model verification was configured but unavailable for this run. Results are single-model only.'
mechanisms.downstream_consumer: integrity_verification_agent (Stage 2.5/4.5 sample checks), devils_advocate reviewers, blind disagreement checkpoints (#518)
mechanisms.terminal_policy_effect: none — cross-model is verification-support, not a gate; its absence is disclosed, never blocking

authority.anchor: Continue with single-model verification — never block the pipeline on cross-model failure
Cross-model verification was configured but unavailable for this run. Results are single-model only.
A `NOT_SEARCHED` result is **not** a transport failure and is handled differently.

mechanisms.failure_class: Compliance checkpoint running outside systematic-review mode (PRISMA-trAIce scope not applicable)
mechanisms.degraded_state: RAISE principles-only evaluation with severity capped at 'warn' — never block (the one registry row with a severity cap, legitimate because compliance already has a native block>warn>info scale; NOT a precedent for score caps elsewhere)
mechanisms.diagnostic_marker: compliance section of the FULL checkpoint dashboard showing principles_only / warn-only evaluation
mechanisms.downstream_consumer: Stage 2.5 / 4.5 integrity gates aggregating compliance decisions via max_severity precedence
mechanisms.terminal_policy_effect: non-SR compliance findings can warn but never block the pipeline; block conditions are SR-mode-only

authority.anchor: | Non-SR mode | RAISE principles_only, warn-only (never block) | Same |
